destination.name: Burgundy
destination.hero_image_credit: Photo by Ile-de-re on Wikimedia Commons, Public domain
destination.hero_image_alt: Cote de Nuits vineyard slopes in Burgundy under morning light
destination.blurb: France's grand-cru heartland, where single-vineyard climats of Pinot Noir and Chardonnay run from Chablis through the Cote d'Or to the Maconnais.
destination.tagline: One thousand climats of Pinot Noir and Chardonnay
destination.overview: Burgundy stretches from the chalky Chablis vineyards through the limestone Cote d'Or to the Cote Chalonnaise and Maconnais, with a UNESCO-listed mosaic of named climats producing the world's benchmark Pinot Noir and Chardonnay.

index.title: Burgundy Wine Region Guide | Cork & Curve
index.description: Explore Burgundy wine estates, sub-appellations and grand cru climats from Chablis and the Cote de Nuits to Meursault, the Cote Chalonnaise and the Maconnais.

vineyards.title: Burgundy Vineyards and Domaines | Cork & Curve
vineyards.description: Discover Burgundy domaines and maisons from Romanee-Conti and Armand Rousseau to Louis Jadot, Drouhin, Leflaive and William Fevre.

neighborhoods.title: Burgundy Sub-Appellations and Wine Areas | Cork & Curve
neighborhoods.description: Burgundy's wine areas, from the Cote de Nuits and Cote de Beaune to Chablis, the Cote Chalonnaise, the Maconnais and the grand-cru communes of the Cote d'Or.

signature-grapes.title: Burgundy Signature Grapes | Cork & Curve
signature-grapes.description: Burgundy grape varieties from Pinot Noir on the Cote d'Or and Chardonnay in Chablis and Meursault to Aligote.

faqs.q: What grapes is Burgundy famous for?
faqs.a: Burgundy is built on two grapes: Pinot Noir for its reds and Chardonnay for its whites. Aligote, Gamay, Cesar and Sauvignon de Saint-Bris play smaller, area-specific roles.

Burgundy uses a four-tier pyramid: Grand Cru at the top, then Premier Cru, then Village (commune) wines, then regional Bourgogne appellations. Classification attaches to the named vineyard, or climat, not the producer.

faqs.q: What is a climat in Burgundy?
faqs.a: A climat is a precisely delimited single vineyard parcel with its own name, history and boundaries. The Climats of the Cote d'Or were inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 2015.

faqs.q: Can you visit the top Burgundy domaines?
faqs.a: Large maisons such as Louis Jadot, Joseph Drouhin, Bouchard Pere et Fils, Albert Bichot and William Fevre run public cellar visits. Many small grand-cru domaines like Romanee-Conti and Leroy sell on allocation and do not receive visitors.
